The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

[[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]][[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]][[Category:11]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808年}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851年}}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶}} ({{ill|884|ja|884年}}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291年}}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} ({{ill|808|ja|808年}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851年}}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} ({{ill|926|ja|926年}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570年}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573年}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674年}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908年}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909年}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851年}}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} ({{ill|926|ja|926年}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570年}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573年}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674年}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908年}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909年}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808年}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851年}}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶}} ({{ill|884|ja|884年}}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291年}}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} ({{ill|808|ja|808年}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851年}}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} ({{ill|926|ja|926年}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570年}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573年}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674年}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908年}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909年}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:New]]

[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]][[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]][[Category:11]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808年}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year }}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶}} ({{ill|884|ja|884年}}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291st year }}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} ({{ill|808|ja|808年}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year }}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} ({{ill|926|ja|926年}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570年}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573年}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674年}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908年}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909年}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:New]]

Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808年}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year }}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶}} ({{ill|884|ja|884th year }}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291st year }}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} ({{ill|808|ja|808年}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year }}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} ({{ill|926|ja|926年}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570年}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year }}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year }}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908年}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909年}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:New]]

At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year }}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} ({{ill|926|ja|926年}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570年}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year }}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year }}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year }} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year }}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:New]]

In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year }} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:New]]

[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]][[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]][[Category:11]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808th year }}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year }}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶}} ({{ill|884|ja|884th year }}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291st year }}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} ({{ill|808|ja|808th year }}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year }}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} ({{ill|926|ja|926th year }}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570th year }} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year }}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year }}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year }} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year }}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]][[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]][[Category:11]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808th year }}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year }}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶}} ({{ill|884|ja|884th year }}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291st year }}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} ({{ill|808|ja|808th year }}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year }}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} ({{ill|926|ja|926th year }}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570th year }} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year }}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year }}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year }} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year }}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570th year }} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year }}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year }}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year }} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year }}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|12=draft}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|12=draft}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|12=draft}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808th year|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=draft}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}. Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|12=draft}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=draft}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|12=draft}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|12=draft}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year|12=draft}}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶|12=draft}} ({{ill|884|ja|884th year|12=draft}}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=draft}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|12=draft}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王|12=draft}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応|12=draft}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291st year|12=draft}}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|12=draft}} ({{ill|808|ja|808th year|12=draft}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|12=draft}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|12=draft}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year|12=draft}}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=draft}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft}} ({{ill|926|ja|926th year|12=draft}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|12=draft}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社|12=draft}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|12=draft}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|12=draft}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year|12=draft}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year|12=draft}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year|12=draft}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|12=draft}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|12=draft}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅|12=draft}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅|12=draft}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線|12=draft}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線|12=draft}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year|12=draft}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|12=draft}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|12=draft}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅|12=draft}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅|12=draft}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線|12=draft}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線|12=draft}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|12=draft}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|12=draft}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|12=draft}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808th year|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=draft}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}. Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|12=draft}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=draft}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|12=draft}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|12=draft}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year|12=draft}}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶|12=draft}} ({{ill|884|ja|884th year|12=draft}}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=draft}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|12=draft}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王|12=draft}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応|12=draft}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291st year|12=draft}}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|12=draft}} ({{ill|808|ja|808th year|12=draft}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|12=draft}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|12=draft}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year|12=draft}}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=draft}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft}} ({{ill|926|ja|926th year|12=draft}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|12=draft}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社|12=draft}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|12=draft}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|12=draft}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year|12=draft}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year|12=draft}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year|12=draft}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|12=draft}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|12=draft}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅|12=draft}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅|12=draft}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線|12=draft}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線|12=draft}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}
