In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year|12=draft}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|12=draft}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|12=draft}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅|12=draft}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅|12=draft}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線|12=draft}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線|12=draft}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|12=draft|12=draft}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=draft|12=draft}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|12=draft|12=draft}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=draft|12=draft}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|12=draft|12=draft}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year|12=draft|12=draft}}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|884|ja|884th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291st year|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|808|ja|808th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|12=draft|12=draft}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year|12=draft|12=draft}}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|926|ja|926th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|12=draft|12=draft}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社|12=draft|12=draft}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft|12=draft}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=draft|12=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=draft|12=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線|12=draft|12=draft}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線|12=draft|12=draft}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=draft|12=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=draft|12=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線|12=draft|12=draft}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線|12=draft|12=draft}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|12=draft|12=draft}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=draft|12=draft}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|12=draft|12=draft}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=draft|12=draft}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|12=draft|12=draft}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year|12=draft|12=draft}}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|884|ja|884th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291st year|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|808|ja|808th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|12=draft|12=draft}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year|12=draft|12=draft}}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|926|ja|926th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|12=draft|12=draft}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社|12=draft|12=draft}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft|12=draft}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=draft|12=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=draft|12=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線|12=draft|12=draft}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線|12=draft|12=draft}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

revision.comment: 30 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:雄琴神社 (大津市) up to rev 98818990
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 雄琴神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
本殿 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[大津市]]雄琴2丁目10-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} |祭神 = 大炊神[[阿保今雄|今雄宿禰命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 伝・[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年（[[808年]]） |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月29日]] |神事 = }} [[ファイル:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鳥居]] [[ファイル:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|[[神紋]]「[[茗荷紋|抱茗荷]]」]] '''雄琴神社'''（おごとじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]雄琴にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[神紋]]は「[[茗荷紋|抱茗荷]]」滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 :* [[阿保今雄|大炊神 今雄宿禰命]]（おおいのかみ いまおすくねのみこと） :*: 文献上では「阿保今雄」・「小槻今雄」とも。[[落別王|於知別命]]（[[垂仁天皇]][[皇子]]）の後裔で、[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）雄琴荘を拝領し[[元慶]]8年（[[884年]]）に歿した。のち当地を治めた[[小槻氏]]（[[官務|官務家]]）の祖。 ; 配祀神 :* 樟本神社：崇道尽敬天皇（[[舎人親王]]）{{harv|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}では[[早良親王]]（諡号は「崇道天皇」）としている。 :* 二峯神社：[[大国主|大己貴命]] : いずれも[[正応]]4年（[[1291年]]）[[相殿]]に[[配祀]]。 == 歴史 == [[大同_(日本)|大同]]3年（[[808年]]）雄琴山上に鎮座したとされる。この時はまだ[[阿保今雄|今雄宿禰]]は祀られておらず、土地神であったと推測されている。 [[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）今雄が雄琴荘を拝領、当地は[[小槻氏]]所領となる。社名・地名の「雄琴」は、今雄宿禰の邸から琴の音が聞こえていたためだともいわれる。今雄の死後、[[延長 (元号)|延長]]4年（[[926年]]）に子・[[小槻当平]]が社殿を造営し、一族の礎を築いた今雄の神霊を「雄琴社大炊神」と称して祭祀した。小槻氏により同市苗鹿にある[[那波加神社]]とともに[[氏神]]として崇敬され、ともに同地にある[[法光寺 (大津市)|法光寺]]の[[鎮守社]]を担ったとされる『日本歴史地名大系 滋賀県の地名』法光寺項。。また鳥居前方にある福領寺は、当社の[[神宮寺]]であった。 [[元亀]]年間（[[1570年]] - [[1573年]]）の兵火で社殿以下が焼亡したが、[[延宝]]2年（[[1674年]]）再建された。 [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）、[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した。 == 境内 == * 本殿 - 一間社[[流造]]で、間口一間、奥行一間三尺。 * 中門 * 拝殿 - [[入母屋造]]で、間口二間・奥行二間。 * 神饌所 * 神輿庫 ファイル:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|境内 ファイル:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|中門 ファイル:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|参道 == 摂末社 == * 樟本神社 * 二峰神社 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：[[倉稲魂命]]、[[市杵島姫命]]、[[水速女命]]摂末社はいずれも境内由緒書を参考にして記載。。 * 牛尾神社 - 祭神：[[大山咋神]][[荒魂]]、[[菅原道真公]]。 * 野神社 - 祭神不詳。 * 地主神社 - 祭神：[[大物主神]]。[[1909年]]（[[明治]]42年）、境内に移転鎮座。 * 霊光社 - 当社縁の人物八木氏の霊や、[[日清戦争]]・[[日露戦争]]・[[日中戦争]]・[[太平洋戦争]]戦没者を祀る。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[滋賀県]][[大津市]]雄琴二丁目10-1 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道 ** [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[湖西線]] [[おごと温泉駅]] （徒歩約10分） * バス ** [[びわ湖浜大津駅]]（[[京阪電気鉄道|京阪]][[京阪石山坂本線|石山坂本線]]・[[京阪京津線|京津線]]）から、[[江若交通|江若バス]]で「北雄琴」バス停下車 （下車後徒歩約7分） '''周辺''' * 福領寺 - 元[[神宮寺]]。[[本尊]]の[[円仁]]作[[阿弥陀如来]]坐像は[[重要文化財]]。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite journal|和書|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |publisher=京都大学数理解析研究所 |volume=1257 |pages=181-185 |hdl=2433/41936 |CRID=1050282677273315968 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[雄琴神社 (壬生町)|雄琴神社]]（[[栃木県]][[壬生町]]） - 当社の分霊を合祀し、同じ社名に改名。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=18 雄琴神社] - 滋賀県神社庁 * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html 雄琴神社] - 大津市歴史博物館 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おことしんしやおおつし}} [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:小槻氏|社おことしんしやおおつし]]

[[1573年]]）の兵火で社殿以下が焼亡したが、[[延宝]]2年（[[1674年]]）再建された。 [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）、[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した。 == 境内 == * 本殿 - 一間社[[流造]]で、間口一間、奥行一間三尺。 * 中門 * 拝殿 - [[入母屋造]]で、間口二間・奥行二間。 * 神饌所 * 神輿庫 ファイル:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|境内 ファイル:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|中門 ファイル:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|参道 == 摂末社 == * 樟本神社 * 二峰神社 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：[[倉稲魂命]]、[[市杵島姫命]]、[[水速女命]]摂末社はいずれも境内由緒書を参考にして記載。。 * 牛尾神社 - 祭神：[[大山咋神]][[荒魂]]、[[菅原道真公]]。 * 野神社 - 祭神不詳。 * 地主神社 - 祭神：[[大物主神]]。[[1909年]]（[[明治]]42年）、境内に移転鎮座。 * 霊光社 - 当社縁の人物八木氏の霊や、[[日清戦争]]・[[日露戦争]]・[[日中戦争]]・[[太平洋戦争]]戦没者を祀る。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[滋賀県]][[大津市]]雄琴二丁目10-1 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道 ** [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[湖西線]] [[おごと温泉駅]] （徒歩約10分） * バス ** [[びわ湖浜大津駅]]（[[京阪電気鉄道|京阪]][[京阪石山坂本線|石山坂本線]]・[[京阪京津線|京津線]]）から、[[江若交通|江若バス]]で「北雄琴」バス停下車 （下車後徒歩約7分） '''周辺''' * 福領寺 - 元[[神宮寺]]。[[本尊]]の[[円仁]]作[[阿弥陀如来]]坐像は[[重要文化財]]。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite journal|和書|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |publisher=京都大学数理解析研究所 |volume=1257 |pages=181-185 |hdl=2433/41936 |CRID=1050282677273315968 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[雄琴神社 (壬生町)|雄琴神社]]（[[栃木県]][[壬生町]]） - 当社の分霊を合祀し、同じ社名に改名。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=18 雄琴神社] - 滋賀県神社庁 * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html 雄琴神社] - 大津市歴史博物館 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おことしんしやおおつし}} [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:小槻氏|社おことしんしやおおつし]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808th year}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year}}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶}} ({{ill|884|ja|884th year}}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291st year}}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} ({{ill|808|ja|808th year}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year}}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} ({{ill|926|ja|926th year}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570th year}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808th year}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year}}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶}} ({{ill|884|ja|884th year}}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291st year}}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同}} ({{ill|808|ja|808th year}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year}}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} ({{ill|926|ja|926th year}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570th year}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|12=draft|12=draft}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=draft|12=draft}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|12=draft|12=draft}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=draft|12=draft}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|12=draft|12=draft}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year|12=draft|12=draft}}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|884|ja|884th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291st year|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|808|ja|808th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|12=draft|12=draft}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year|12=draft|12=draft}}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|926|ja|926th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|12=draft|12=draft}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社|12=draft|12=draft}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft|12=draft}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=draft|12=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=draft|12=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線|12=draft|12=draft}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線|12=draft|12=draft}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}}

In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=draft|12=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=draft|12=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|12=draft|12=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線|12=draft|12=draft}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線|12=draft|12=draft}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|884|ja|884th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|808|ja|808th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|851|ja|851st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|926|ja|926th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]]
