era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzō|qq=}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|qq=}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russo-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu City|qq=}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogoto Onsen Station|qq=}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|qq=}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Electric Railway|qq=}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|qq=}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Keishin Line|qq=}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former {{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūji|qq=}}. The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amida Nyorai|qq=}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu City|qq=}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami|qq=}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abo Imao|qq=}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Founded = Legendary: {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|qq=}} 3rd Year ({{ill|808|ja|808年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/808|qq=}}) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzō|qq=}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 29|qq=}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|qq=}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|qq=}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|qq=}} (son of {{ill|Suinin Tennō|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suinin Tennō|qq=}}) who, in the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} ({{ill|851|ja|851年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/851|qq=}}), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of {{ill|Genkei|ja|元慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkei|qq=}} ({{ill|884|ja|884年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/884|qq=}}). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}}) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|qq=}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as {{ill|Sararapōnō|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sararapōnō|qq=}} with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of {{ill|Seiō|ja|正応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiō|qq=}} ({{ill|1291|ja|1291年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1291|qq=}}). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|qq=}} ({{ill|808|ja|808年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/808|qq=}}). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|qq=}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of {{ill|Ninju|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} ({{ill|851|ja|851年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/851|qq=}}), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} ({{ill|926|ja|926年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/926|qq=}}), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|qq=}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Chinju Jinja|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju Jinja|qq=}}) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|qq=}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ({{ill|Jingūji|ja|神宮寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingūji|qq=}}). During the Genkō era ({{ill|1570|ja|1570年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} – {{ill|1573|ja|1573年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1573|qq=}}), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=}} ({{ill|1674|ja|1674年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1674|qq=}}). In {{ill|1908|ja|1908年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1908|qq=}} (the 41st year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the {{ill|Ryūzō|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūzō|qq=}} style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|qq=}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in {{ill|1909|ja|1909年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}}, the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russo-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}. == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Ōtsu City|ja|大津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu City|qq=}} Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Ogoto Onsen Station|ja|おごと温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogoto Onsen Station|qq=}} (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From {{ill|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|ja|びわ湖浜大津駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station|qq=}} (served by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Electric Railway|qq=}} on the {{ill|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|ja|京阪石山坂本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line|qq=}} / {{ill|Keihan Keishin Line|ja|京阪京津線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Keishin Line|qq=}}), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former The principal image is a seated {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amida Nyorai|qq=}} by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami|qq=}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abo Imao|qq=}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Founded = Legendary: [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] 3rd Year ([[:en:AD 808|808]]) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|qq=}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|qq=}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|qq=}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|qq=}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|qq=}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|qq=}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|qq=}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]). During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]). In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|qq=}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]]

The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] [[Category:大津市の神社]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami|qq=}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abo Imao|qq=}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Founded = Legendary: [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] 3rd Year ([[:en:AD 808|808]]) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|qq=}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|qq=}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|qq=}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|qq=}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|qq=}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|qq=}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|qq=}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]). During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]). In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|qq=}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:小槻氏]]

Main Hall | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami|qq=}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abo Imao|qq=}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Founded = Legendary: [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] 3rd Year ([[:en:AD 808|808]]) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|qq=}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|qq=}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|qq=}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|qq=}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|qq=}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|qq=}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|qq=}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]). During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]). In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|qq=}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:小槻氏]] [[Category:Chinjusha]]

Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|qq=}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|qq=}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|qq=}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|qq=}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]). During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]). In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|qq=}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:小槻氏]] [[Category:Chinjusha]]

Main Hall | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami|qq=}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abo Imao|qq=}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Founded = Legendary: [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] 3rd Year ([[:en:AD 808|808]]) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|qq=}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|qq=}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|qq=}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|qq=}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|qq=}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|qq=}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|qq=}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]). During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]). In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|qq=}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:小槻氏]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Ogoto-jinja}}

At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]). During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]). In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 to 荒魂・和魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinju Shrines]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:小槻氏]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Ogoto-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = [[File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abo Imao|qq=}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Founded = Legendary: [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] 3rd Year ([[:en:AD 808|808]]) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo }} [[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”]] '''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 家紋}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。. == Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祖別命}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}} (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]). == History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit. In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|qq=}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]). During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]). In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ}}Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents. * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. == Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 '''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting) '''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name. == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|雄琴神社 (大津市)|version=98818990|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)]] [[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[sn:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] [[ja:雄琴神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:小槻氏]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Commons category|Ogoto-jinja}}
