[[ja:雄琴神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Otsuki Clan]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Ogoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11659231}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ogoto-jinja]][[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11659231]] Wikidata name: Ogoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abo Imao|qq=|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = Legendary: [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] 3rd Year ([[:en:AD 808|808]]) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo | location = {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.094075|N|135.8971333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”]]
'''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 家紋|1=mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q600964}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”<ref>滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祖別命|1=Ojiwake no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620549}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}</ref> (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]).
== History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit.
In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|qq=|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|qq=|1=法光寺|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555025}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]).
During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]).
In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse
<gallery> File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path </gallery>
== Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}<ref>Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents.</ref> * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].
== Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1
'''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting)
'''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property.

Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 家紋|1=mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q600964}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”<ref>滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。</ref>.

At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit.

During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]).
In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse
<gallery> File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path </gallery>
== Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}<ref>Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents.</ref> * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].
== Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1
'''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting)
'''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ogoto-jinja]][[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659231]] Wikidata name: Ogoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abo Imao|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = Legendary: [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] 3rd Year ([[:en:AD 808|808]]) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo | location = {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.094075|N|135.8971333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”]]
'''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 家紋|1=mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q600964}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”<ref>滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祖別命|1=Ojiwake no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620549}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}</ref> (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]).
== History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit.
In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|1=法光寺|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555025}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]).
During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]).
In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse

== Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}<ref>Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents.</ref> * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].

'''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 家紋|1=mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q600964}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”<ref>滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祖別命|1=Ojiwake no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620549}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}</ref> (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]).
== History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit.
In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|1=法光寺|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555025}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]).
During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]).
In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse
<gallery> File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path </gallery>
== Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}<ref>Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents.</ref> * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].
== Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1
'''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting)
'''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property.

'''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 家紋|1=mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q600964}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”<ref>滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祖別命|1=Ojiwake no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620549}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}</ref> (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]).
== History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit.
In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|1=法光寺|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555025}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]).
During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]).
In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ogoto-jinja]][[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659231]] Wikidata name: Ogoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abo Imao|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = Legendary: [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] 3rd Year ([[:en:AD 808|808]]) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo | location = {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.094075|N|135.8971333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”]]
'''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 家紋|1=mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q600964}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”<ref>滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祖別命|1=Ojiwake no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620549}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}</ref> (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]).
== History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit.
In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|1=法光寺|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555025}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]).
During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]).
In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse
<gallery> File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path </gallery>
== Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}<ref>Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents.</ref> * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].
== Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1
'''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting)
'''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property.
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}}
== Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name.
== External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History

After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|1=法光寺|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555025}} in the same area. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}<ref>Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents.</ref> * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].
== Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1
'''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting)
'''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ogoto-jinja]][[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659231]] Wikidata name: Ogoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abo Imao|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = Legendary: [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] 3rd Year ([[:en:AD 808|808]]) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo | location = {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.094075|N|135.8971333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”]]
'''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 家紋|1=mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q600964}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”<ref>滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祖別命|1=Ojiwake no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620549}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}</ref> (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]).
== History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit.
In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|1=法光寺|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555025}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]).
During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]).
In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse

(Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].

Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}</ref> (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]).
== History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit.
In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|1=法光寺|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555025}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]).
During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]).
In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse
<gallery> File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path </gallery>
== Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}<ref>Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents.</ref> * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].
== Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1
'''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting)
'''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property.

'''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 家紋|1=mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q600964}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”<ref>滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祖別命|1=Ojiwake no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620549}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}</ref> (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]).
== History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit.
In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|1=法光寺|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555025}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]).
During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]).
In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system.

(Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ogoto-jinja]][[ja:雄琴神社 (大津市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11659231]] Wikidata name: Ogoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Ogoto Jinja (雄琴神社) | image = File:雄琴神社 本殿.JPG | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|38.67|N|135|53|49.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=雄琴神社}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōinokami|ja|大炊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Abo Imao|ja|阿保今雄|lt=Imao Sukune|lt_ja=今雄宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abo Imao|1=Abo no Imao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657229}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = Legendary: [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] 3rd Year ([[:en:AD 808|808]]) | Main Hall = Single-chamber shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = Japan Tokyo | location = {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.094075|N|135.8971333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:雄琴神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|Shinmon “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”]]
'''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 家紋|1=mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q600964}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”<ref>滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == ; Primary Enshrined Deity :* {{ill|Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja|大炊神 今雄宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōinokami Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* In literature, he is also referred to as “Abo Imao” or “Kotsuki Imao.” He is a descendant of {{ill|Ochibetsuō|ja|於知別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochibetsuō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祖別命|1=Ojiwake no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620549}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin Tennō]]) who, in the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), received the fief of Ogoto-shō and died in the 8th year of [[:en:Gangyō|Genkei]] ([[:en:884|884]]). Later, he was revered as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]]) of the area by the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. ; Secondary Enshrined Deities (Haishōjin) :* Kusamoto Jinja: {{ill|Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja|崇道尽敬天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdōjinkō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}</ref> (in some texts also as [[:en:Prince Sawara|Sararapōnō]] with the posthumous name “Sūdō Tennō”). :* Futamine Jinja: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} : Both were enshrined in the side shrines (【相殿】) in the 4th year of [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Seiō]] ([[:en:1291|1291]]).
== History == It is said that the shrine was established on the summit of Ogoto Mountain in the 3rd year of [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daidō]] ([[:en:AD 808|808]]). At that time, {{ill|Imao Sukune|ja|今雄宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was not yet enshrined and was presumed to be the local land spirit.
In the 1st year of [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] ([[:en:851|851]]), Imao received the fief of Ogoto-shō, and the area came under the control of the {{ill|Kotsuki Clan|ja|小槻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Clan|1=Ozuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462133}}. It is said that the name “Ogoto” originates from the sound of a koto heard from Imao Sukune’s residence. After his death, in the 4th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} ([[:en:926|926]]), his son, {{ill|Kotsuki Atohira|ja|小槻当平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuki Atohira|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, constructed the shrine building and enshrined Imao’s divine spirit under the name “Ogoto-sha Ōinokami.” The Kotsuki clan revered him as their tutelary deity along with the tutelary shrine at Naiga, Ōtsu (【那波加神社】), and he was said to have served as the guardian shrine ([[:en:Chinjusha|Chinju Jinja]]) for {{ill|Hōkōji|ja|法光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkōji|1=法光寺|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555025}} in the same area. Additionally, Fukuryōji, located in front of the torii, once served as the shrine’s temple ([[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]).
During the Genkō era ([[:en:1570|1570]] – [[:en:1573|1573]]), the shrine complex, including the main hall, was destroyed by warfare, but it was rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] ([[:en:1674|1674]]).
In [[:en:1908|1908]] (the 41st year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era), it was classified as a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – A single-chamber shrine constructed in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] style, with an entrance width of one ken and a depth of one ken three shaku. * Middle Gate * Haiden (Worship Hall) – Built in the [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style, with an entrance width of two ken and a depth of two ken. * Shinsenjo (Hall for Divine Offerings) * Mikoshi Storehouse
<gallery> File:雄琴神社 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:雄琴神社 拝所.JPG|Middle Gate File:雄琴神社 参道.JPG|Approach Path </gallery>
== Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == * Kusamoto Jinja * Futamine Jinja * Inari Jinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurainetama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Mizuhayome|ja|水速女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhayome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}<ref>Subordinate shrines are recorded based on the shrine’s own historical documents.</ref> * Ushio Jinja – Enshrined deities: [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuzume-no-Kami]], {{ill|Aratama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. * No Jinja – Enshrined deity unknown. * Jishu Jinja – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. (Relocated and enshrined in the shrine grounds in [[:en:1909|1909]], the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era.) * Reikōsha – Enshrines the spirits of members of the Yagi family associated with the shrine, as well as the war dead from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].
== Local Information == '''Location''' * [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu City]] Ogoto 2-chōme 10-1
'''Access''' * Rail ** {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]]: [[:en:Ogoto-onsen Station|Ogoto Onsen Station]] (approximately 10 minutes on foot) * Bus ** From [[:en:Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station|Biwako Hama Ōtsu Station]] (served by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] on the [[:en:Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line]] / [[:en:Keihan Keishin Line|Keihan Keishin Line]]), take an Ewaki Bus to the “Kita Ogoto” bus stop (approximately 7 minutes on foot after alighting)
'''Surroundings''' * Fukuryōji – Former [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingūji]]. The principal image is a seated [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] by Ennin, designated as an Important Cultural Property.
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{cite journal|lang=ja|author=田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二 |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=算博士小槻今雄について (数学史の研究) |journal=数理解析研究所講究録 |ISSN=1880-2818 |volume=1257 |pages=181–185 |ref={{harvid|田中延佳, 田村三郎, 吉田柳二|2002}}}}
== Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Mibu Town)|Ogoto Jinja]] (in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Mibu Town) – A branch shrine enshrining part of the deity’s spirit, renamed to share the same shrine name.
== External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode=view=1&view:oid=18 Ogoto Jinja] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/jiten/data/036.html Ogoto Jinja] - Ōtsu City Museum of History

'''Ogoto Jinja''' (雄琴神社【おごとじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Ogoto, Ōtsu, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}). The shrine crest ({{ill|Shinmon|ja|神紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 家紋|1=mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q600964}}) is “{{ill|Myōka Mon|ja|抱茗荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōka Mon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”<ref>滋賀県神社庁ホームページより。</ref>.
